content.problemStatement:
You are given an array AAA of length NNN.

A pair (i,j)(i, j)(i,j) (1≤i<j≤N)(1\le i\lt j \le N)(1≤i<j≤N) is said to be good if gcd(Ai,Aj)=lcm(Ai,Aj)gcd(A_i, A_j) = lcm(A_i, A_j)gcd(Ai​,Aj​)=lcm(Ai​,Aj​). Here gcdgcdgcd denotes the greatest common divisor and lcmlcmlcm denotes least common multiple.

Find the total number of good pairs present in the given array.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
The first line of each test case contains an integer NNN - the length of the array AAA.
The second line of each test case contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,…,ANA_1,A_2,\ldots,A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​.


Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the total number of such good pairs possible in the given array.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 3⋅1053 \cdot 10^53⋅105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: No good pair is present.
Test case 222: The good pairs (i,j)(i, j)(i,j) are: (2,3),(3,4),(2, 3), (3, 4),(2,3),(3,4), and (2,4)(2, 4)(2,4). To elaborate, gcd(A2,A3)=lcm(A2,A3)=5gcd(A_2, A_3) = lcm(A_2, A_3) = 5gcd(A2​,A3​)=lcm(A2​,A3​)=5.
Test case 333: The good pairs (i,j)(i, j)(i,j) are: (1,4),(2,3),(5,6),(6,7),(1, 4), (2, 3), (5, 6), (6, 7),(1,4),(2,3),(5,6),(6,7), and (5,7)(5, 7)(5,7). To elaborate, gcd(A1,A4)=lcm(A1,A4)=2gcd(A_1, A_4) = lcm(A_1, A_4) = 2gcd(A1​,A4​)=lcm(A1​,A4​)=2.
Test case 444: The good pair (i,j)(i, j)(i,j) are: (1,2),(1, 2),(1,2), and (3,4)(3, 4)(3,4). To elaborate, gcd(A3,A4)=lcm(A3,A4)=18gcd(A_3, A_4) = lcm(A_3, A_4) = 18gcd(A3​,A4​)=lcm(A3​,A4​)=18.
Test case 555: No good pair is present.
